ActionBar.description: A horizontal toolbar of icon buttons, optionally separated into groups by dividers; buttons that don't fit the available width collapse into an overflow menu.

children.description: The buttons, dividers, groups, and menus contained in the toolbar.

size.description: The size of the toolbar's buttons.

flush.description: Whether the toolbar sits flush with its container's edge, with no outer padding.

accessibility.description: Accessible name for the toolbar, required so assistive technologies can identify it. Maps to `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby` when naming by another element's id.

ActionBar.IconButton.description: An icon-only button inside an action-bar toolbar; it triggers an action when activated and carries a required label for assistive technologies.

icon.description: The icon displayed as the button's content.

action.description: The action performed when the button is activated.

accessibility.description: Label describing the button's function for assistive technologies; also serves as its tooltip text. Required — an icon-only button must be labeled.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled; it stays focusable with `aria-disabled` applied.

ActionBar.Divider.description: A vertical separator that splits the toolbar's buttons into visually distinct groups; it is preserved when buttons collapse into the overflow menu.

ActionBar.Group.description: Groups related toolbar buttons so they stay visually together and collapse into the overflow menu as a unit.

children.description: The related buttons grouped together.

ActionBar.Menu.description: A toolbar button that opens a dropdown menu of actions when activated; menu entries may carry icons, trailing text, and nested submenus, and may be separated by dividers.

icon.description: The icon displayed on the menu button (references an `Icon`).

accessibility.description: Accessible name for the menu button, required. Maps to `aria-label` (or `aria-labelledby`).

items.description: The entries shown in the dropdown menu (see the item schema below).

type.description: The kind of entry: an action item (default) or a separator.

action.description: The action performed when the item is selected.

disabled.description: Whether the item is disabled and cannot be selected.

leadingVisual.description: An icon shown before the item's text.

trailingVisual.description: An icon or a short text string shown after the item's text.

variant.description: The item's visual style; `danger` marks a destructive action.

items.description: Nested entries rendered as a submenu.

type.description: The kind of entry: an action item (default) or a separator.

action.description: The action performed when the item is selected.

disabled.description: Whether the item is disabled and cannot be selected.

leadingVisual.description: An icon shown before the item's text.

trailingVisual.description: An icon or a short text string shown after the item's text.

variant.description: The item's visual style; `danger` marks a destructive action.

overflowIcon.description: The icon shown for this menu when the toolbar collapses it into the overflow menu; defaults to the menu button's own `icon`, or `'none'` to show no icon.

ActionList.description: A vertically stacked list of actions or selectable options, optionally organized into labeled groups and separated by dividers.

children.description: The items, groups, dividers, and heading contained in the list.

variant.description: How items are offset from the list's edges.

selectionVariant.description: Whether one or many items may be selected, and how a selected item is indicated (checkmark, radio button, or checkbox).

showDividers.description: Whether a divider is shown above each item.

role.description: The ARIA role describing the list's function, e.g. `listbox` or `menu`.

disableFocusZone.description: Whether the list's built-in keyboard arrow-key navigation is turned off.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the list container; the choices are display-equivalent and differ only in semantic/landmark identity.

accessibility.description: Accessible label/description for the list, for assistive technologies.

ActionList.Item.description: A single selectable or actionable row in an action list, holding a label and optional leading/trailing visuals, a secondary description, and a trailing action.

children.description: The item's label plus its leading/trailing visuals, description, and trailing action.

selected.description: Whether the item is selected; two-way bound — a click writes the new state back. Meaningful only when the list or group sets a `selectionVariant`.

action.description: An effect performed when the item is selected — a local function or a server event.

active.description: Whether the item is the currently-active one (e.g. the current page); at most one item should be active.

variant.description: The item's style; `danger` marks a destructive action.

size.description: The item's block size.

disabled.description: Whether the item is disabled and cannot be clicked, selected, or navigated to.

loading.description: Whether the item is in a loading state.

inactiveText.description: Text explaining why the item is temporarily unavailable; its presence marks the item inactive.

role.description: The ARIA role describing the item's function, e.g. `option` or `menuitemradio`.

ActionList.LinkItem.description: An action-list row that navigates to a URL when clicked, rendered as a link.

children.description: The link item's label plus its leading/trailing visuals and description.

href.description: The URL the item navigates to when clicked.

active.description: Whether this is the currently-active item (e.g. the current page); at most one item should be active.

inactiveText.description: Text explaining why the item is temporarily unavailable; its presence marks the item inactive.

variant.description: The item's style; `danger` marks a destructive item.

size.description: The item's block size.

target.description: Whether the URL opens in the same tab (`_self`) or a new tab (`_blank`).

ActionList.Group.description: A labeled section grouping related items within an action list, optionally with its own selection mode.

children.description: The items contained in the group.

variant.description: The group's style; `filled` adds a background color and top/bottom borders.

auxiliaryText.description: Secondary text providing additional information about the group.

selectionVariant.description: Whether one or many items in this group may be selected, overriding the list's setting; `false` disables selection for this group.

role.description: The ARIA role describing the function of the list inside the group.

ActionList.GroupHeading.description: A heading that labels a group of items within an action list, optionally with a trailing action beside the label.

children.description: The heading label and its optional trailing action.

variant.description: The heading's style; `filled` adds a background color and top/bottom borders.

auxiliaryText.description: Secondary text providing additional information about the group.

visuallyHidden.description: Whether the heading is hidden visually but kept available to assistive technologies.

as.description: The heading level in the document outline.

ActionList.LeadingVisual.description: A visual, typically an icon, shown before an item's label.

children.description: The visual content shown before the item's label — typically an icon.

ActionList.TrailingVisual.description: A visual, typically an icon, counter, or keyboard hint, shown after an item's label.

children.description: The visual content shown after the item's label — typically an icon, counter, or keyboard hint.

ActionList.Description.description: Secondary text describing an item, shown beside or below the item's label.

variant.description: Whether the description sits beside the label (`inline`) or below it (`block`).

truncate.description: Whether an inline description truncates its text on overflow.

ActionList.Divider.description: A visual separator between items or groups in an action list.

ActionList.TrailingAction.description: A secondary action, shown after an item's content, that runs an action when clicked or navigates when given a link target.

icon.description: The icon displayed as the action's content.

label.description: The action's accessible name, and its visible text when rendered as a plain button.

action.description: The action performed when the button is activated — a local function or a server event. Applies in button mode.

as.description: Whether the control renders as a button or a link.

href.description: The URL navigated to when rendered as a link (`as: "a"`).

loading.description: Whether the action is in a loading state (button mode).

ActionList.Heading.description: A heading that labels the entire action list.

as.description: The heading level in the document outline.

size.description: The heading's visual size, independent of its outline level.

visuallyHidden.description: Whether the heading is hidden visually but kept available to assistive technologies.

Text.description: Displays a run of text with configurable typography.

as.description: The HTML element to render.

size.description: Font size.

Button.description: An interactive button that triggers an action when clicked.

child.description: The ID of the component used as the button's label. Use a Text component; do not define it inline. Optional: omit it when `icon` is set (the button renders icon-only).

action.description: The action performed when the button is clicked.

variant.description: The visual style. 'primary' marks the main call-to-action; 'danger' a destructive action.

size.description: The button size.

alignContent.description: How the content is aligned within the button.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled and cannot be clicked.

loading.description: Whether the button shows a loading state.

inactive.description: Whether the button looks disabled but still accepts interaction.

count.description: A count value displayed alongside the label.

block.description: Whether the button fills the width of its container.

labelWrap.description: Whether the label wraps to multiple lines when it is longer than the button width.

loadingAnnouncement.description: Text announced to assistive technologies while the button is loading.

accessibility.description: Accessibility attributes (label, description) for assistive technologies.

icon.description: The ID of an icon component that renders the button in icon-only mode. When set, the label and any visuals are not shown. Reference an Icon component; do not define it inline.

leadingVisual.description: The ID of a component shown before the label, typically an Icon. Reference the component; do not define it inline.

trailingVisual.description: The ID of a component shown after the label, typically an Icon. Reference the component; do not define it inline.

trailingAction.description: The ID of a component rendered as the last element in the button, after any trailing visual. Reference the component; do not define it inline.

IconButton.description: An interactive icon-only button that initiates an action when activated; it carries a required text label for assistive technologies.

icon.description: The icon displayed as the button's content.

action.description: The action performed when the button is activated.

accessibility.description: Label for assistive technologies, and the button's tooltip text when no `description` is set. Required — an icon-only button must be labeled.

description.description: Descriptive text shown in the button's tooltip; when set, the accessibility label names the button and this describes it.

variant.description: The visual style; `primary` marks the main call-to-action, `danger` a destructive action, `invisible`/`link` for minimal contexts.

size.description: The button's size.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled and cannot be activated.

loading.description: Whether the button is in a loading state, showing a progress indicator in place of the icon.

inactive.description: Whether the button appears disabled but remains interactive.

loadingAnnouncement.description: Text announced to assistive technologies while loading.

block.description: Whether the button expands to fill its container's width.

keybindingHint.description: A keyboard-shortcut hint shown in the tooltip; a single chord or a sequence of chords.

tooltipDirection.description: Which side the tooltip is anchored to.

Icon.description: A vector icon, selected by name from a fixed icon set, used to visually communicate meaning, state, or action.

name.description: The name of the icon to display, from the icon set.

size.description: The rendered size of the icon.

fill.description: The icon's semantic color role; `default` inherits the surrounding text color.

accessibility.description: Label for assistive technologies; an unlabeled icon is decorative and hidden from them.

Link.description: A hyperlink that navigates to a URL when clicked, rendered as styled link text.

href.description: The URL the link navigates to when clicked.

muted.description: Renders the link in a less prominent shade.

inline.description: Underlines the link, for links positioned within a block of text.

target.description: Whether the linked URL opens in the same tab (`_self`) or a new tab (`_blank`).

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies, e.g. a fuller link description than the visible text.

Heading.description: Displays a section heading with a selectable semantic level and configurable visual size. A non-interactive display primitive.

as.description: The heading element to render; selects the semantic level in the document outline, independent of visual size.

variant.description: The visual size of the heading, independent of the semantic level.

Label.description: Displays a small badge of contextual metadata — such as a status or category — with a selectable color variant and size. A non-interactive display primitive.

variant.description: The color scheme of the label, signaling the kind of metadata (e.g. success, danger, attention).

size.description: How large the label is rendered.

BranchName.description: A styled label that displays the name of a branch, rendered as a link when it navigates to a destination or as plain text when the reference is contextual.

text.description: The branch name text to display.

href.description: The URL the branch name links to; clicking the label navigates there.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the label: an anchor when the branch name navigates, a plain span when the reference is contextual text.

RelativeTime.description: Displays a timestamp as localized relative time (e.g. "3 days ago"), an elapsed duration, or an absolute localized date, depending on format and distance from now. A non-interactive display primitive.

datetime.description: The moment in time to display, as an ISO 8601 datetime string.

format.description: How to present the time: `auto` switches from relative to an absolute date past the threshold; `relative` is always relative; `duration` shows the elapsed duration; `datetime` shows an absolute localized date.

formatStyle.description: The verbosity of the formatted output (e.g. "3 days ago" vs "3d").

tense.description: Which tense to render relative time in.

precision.description: The smallest unit shown when displaying elapsed/duration time.

threshold.description: ISO 8601 duration beyond which display switches from relative to an absolute date.

prefix.description: Text prepended to an absolute date (e.g. "on Jan 1, 2026").

second.description: How the seconds part of an absolute date is rendered.

minute.description: How the minutes part of an absolute date is rendered.

hour.description: How the hours part of an absolute date is rendered.

weekday.description: How the weekday part of an absolute date is rendered.

day.description: How the day part of an absolute date is rendered.

month.description: How the month part of an absolute date is rendered.

year.description: How the year part of an absolute date is rendered.

timeZoneName.description: Whether and how the time-zone name is appended to an absolute date.

noTitle.description: Suppresses the hover tooltip showing the full formatted date.

StateLabel.description: A badge that displays the status of an item such as an issue or pull request, with an icon and color treatment matched to the state.

text.description: The status text to display inside the badge.

status.description: The state to display; selects the badge's icon and color treatment. Covers issue and pull-request states, security-alert states, and generic open/closed/archived.

size.description: The badge's size.

CounterLabel.description: A badge that displays a numeric count, used to indicate a quantity such as the number of items in a list or notifications.

variant.description: The visual emphasis of the counter: `primary` renders a filled high-contrast badge, `secondary` a subtle muted one.

Avatar.description: An image that represents a user or a non-human entity such as an organization, team, or bot — typically a profile picture.

src.description: URL of the avatar image to display.

alt.description: Alternative text describing the avatar for assistive technologies, needed when no adjacent name identifies it.

size.description: The avatar's width and height in pixels.

square.description: Renders a square avatar instead of a circular one; circles represent people, squares represent non-human entities like organizations, teams, or bots.

Spinner.description: An indeterminate loading indicator for processes with unknown or variable duration.

size.description: The width and height of the spinner.

srText.description: Text conveyed to assistive technologies while the spinner is shown; describes what is loading. `null` suppresses it when a visible text node elsewhere announces the loading state.

delay.description: Delay before the spinner becomes visible (`short` ≈ 300ms, `long` ≈ 1000ms), so briefly-loading content never flashes a spinner.

Token.description: A compact representation of an object, typically used to show a collection of related metadata. Optionally removable via a remove action.

text.description: The text label inside the token.

leadingVisual.description: A component rendered before the token's text (e.g. an icon). Not rendered at the small size.

as.description: The element the token renders as: a static span, a button, or a link.

removeAction.description: The action performed when the user removes the token — via the remove button, or Backspace/Delete while focused. Omitting it renders no remove button.

hideRemoveButton.description: Whether the remove button is hidden; keyboard removal remains available when a remove action is set.

isSelected.description: Whether the token is in a selected state.

size.description: How large the token is rendered.

disabled.description: Whether the token is disabled and non-interactive.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies.

IssueLabelToken.description: A compact token representing an issue label, filled with the label's corresponding color. Optionally removable via a remove action.

text.description: The text label inside the token.

fillColor.description: The color corresponding to the label the token represents; the token's fill and text derive from it.

as.description: The element the token renders as: a static span, a button, or a link.

removeAction.description: The action performed when the user removes the token — via the remove button, or Backspace/Delete while focused. Omitting it renders no remove button.

hideRemoveButton.description: Whether the remove button is hidden; keyboard removal remains available when a remove action is set.

isSelected.description: Whether the token is in a selected state.

size.description: How large the token is rendered.

disabled.description: Whether the token is disabled and non-interactive.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies.

Checkbox.description: A checkbox input for selecting or deselecting a single option; supports checked, unchecked, and indeterminate states.

checked.description: Whether the checkbox is checked. Two-way bound: user toggles write the new value back to the bound data-model path.

indeterminate.description: Shows the partial-selection state (e.g. a select-all over a partly-selected list). While set, it overrides the checked appearance and is conveyed as "mixed" to assistive technologies.

disabled.description: Whether the checkbox is inactive and cannot be toggled.

required.description: Whether the checkbox must be checked; conveyed to assistive technologies.

validationStatus.description: Informs assistive technologies of validity only; an individual checkbox has no validation styles.

value.description: A unique identifier for this checkbox input, never shown to the user; corresponds to the native form-submission value and has no effect outside one.

accessibility.description: Label/description for assistive technologies — the checkbox renders no visible label of its own.

ProgressBar.description: A horizontal bar that visualizes the completion percentage of a task or process, either as a single filled segment or as multiple colored segments.

progress.description: The completion percentage, 0–100. Mutually exclusive with `segments`; omitting both renders an empty track.

segments.description: The segments of a multi-segment bar, each with its own percentage and color. Mutually exclusive with `progress`.

progress.description: The completion percentage of this segment, 0–100.

bg.description: The semantic color role of this segment.

label.description: The accessible name of that segment (maps to the segment's `aria-label`).

bg.description: The semantic color role of the filled segment.

inline.description: Whether the bar renders inline with surrounding content instead of as a block.

animated.description: Whether the filled segment is animated. Applies to the single-`progress` form.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label naming what is progressing, for assistive technologies.

Radio.description: A radio input for selecting a single option from a mutually exclusive group.

value.description: A unique value identifying this option within its group; never shown to the user.

name.description: The group key; radios sharing a `name` are mutually exclusive.

disabled.description: Whether the radio is disabled and cannot be selected.

required.description: Whether a selection in this radio's group must be made before the containing form can be submitted.

action.description: The action performed when this radio becomes selected.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies; a radio renders no visible label of its own.

ToggleSwitch.description: A switch that immediately turns a setting on or off, showing its current state as a short status text.

checked.description: Whether the switch is on. Two-way bound: toggling writes the new value back to the bound data-model path.

action.description: Performed when the user toggles the switch — for settings that must reach the agent or run a local effect at flip time.

disabled.description: Whether the switch is disabled and cannot be toggled.

loading.description: Whether the switch's value is being computed or saved; blocks toggling meanwhile.

size.description: The size of the switch; `small` for dense layouts.

statusLabelPosition.description: Whether the on/off status text appears before or after the switch.

buttonLabelOn.description: Status text shown when the switch is on; customize only for a more specific context (e.g. "Show").

buttonLabelOff.description: Status text shown when the switch is off; customize only for a more specific context (e.g. "Hide").

loadingLabel.description: Text announced to assistive technologies while loading. Non-visual.

loadingLabelDelay.description: Milliseconds before the loading text is announced to assistive technologies. Non-visual.

accessibility.description: Label naming the setting the switch controls — the switch renders no visible label of its own, and an unnamed on/off control is meaningless to assistive technologies.

Textarea.description: A multiline plain-text input for entering longer free-form text; supports validation states and an optional character counter.

value.description: The multiline text content. Two-way bound: user edits write back to the bound data-model path.

placeholder.description: Hint text shown while the textarea is empty.

disabled.description: Whether the textarea is inactive and cannot be edited.

required.description: Whether a value is required; conveyed to assistive technologies.

validationStatus.description: Styles the textarea to reflect the current validation state.

block.description: Expands the textarea to fill the parent's width.

resize.description: Which directions the user may resize the textarea in.

characterLimit.description: Optional character limit; shows a live character counter below the textarea and applies error styling when exceeded.

minHeight.description: Minimum height of the textarea in pixels; bounds user resizing.

maxHeight.description: Maximum height of the textarea in pixels; bounds user resizing.

accessibility.description: Label/description for assistive technologies — the textarea renders no visible label of its own.

TextInput.description: A single-line plain-text input for entering short free-form text; supports leading/trailing visuals, validation states, a loading indicator, and an optional character counter.

value.description: The text content. Two-way bound: user edits write back to the bound data-model path.

placeholder.description: Hint text shown while the input is empty.

disabled.description: Whether the input is inactive and cannot be edited.

required.description: Whether a value is required; conveyed to assistive technologies.

validationStatus.description: Styles the input to reflect the current validation state. Bindable to a data-model path so an agent can drive the validation state.

type.description: The kind of text input; controls masking, keyboard, and input semantics.

loading.description: Whether to show a loading indicator inside the input.

loaderPosition.description: Where the loading indicator renders; `auto` places it trailing unless a leading visual is present.

loaderText.description: Screen-reader text conveying the loading state.

leadingVisual.description: A visual rendered inside the input before the typing area.

trailingVisual.description: A visual rendered inside the input after the typing area.

trailingAction.description: An action button rendered inside the input at the trailing edge (e.g. a clear or show/hide toggle). References a `TextInput.Action` (shipped as a sibling leaf; see `textinput-action.md`).

size.description: The input's overall size.

block.description: Expands the input to fill the parent's width.

monospace.description: Renders the text in a monospace font.

characterLimit.description: Optional character limit; shows a live character counter below the input and applies error styling when exceeded.

accessibility.description: Label/description for assistive technologies — the input renders no visible label of its own.

TextInput.Action.description: An icon action button rendered inside a text input at its trailing edge — triggers an action such as clearing the field or toggling password visibility.

action.description: The action performed when the button is clicked.

icon.description: The icon rendered inside the button.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled and cannot be clicked.

tooltipDirection.description: Position of the tooltip relative to the button.

accessibility.description: Accessible label for the button; also shown as the button's tooltip text.

SkeletonBox.description: A shimmering placeholder box that reserves space for non-text content, such as an image, while it loads.

height.description: Height of the placeholder box; any CSS height value.

width.description: Width of the placeholder box; any CSS width value. Fills its container when unset.

delay.description: Delay before the placeholder becomes visible (`short` ≈ 300ms, `long` ≈ 1000ms), so briefly-loading content never flashes a skeleton.

Truncate.description: Shortens overflowing text with an ellipsis, constrained to a maximum width.

title.description: The full untruncated text, exposed to assistive technologies and shown when the text is hovered.

inline.description: Displays the text inline (as inline-block, top-aligned) so it can sit alongside other content.

expandable.description: Reveals the full text when hovered.

maxWidth.description: The maximum width of the text before it truncates; any CSS width value.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the truncated text; the choices are display-equivalent.

KeybindingHint.description: A styled hint indicating an available keyboard shortcut, displaying the keys to press.

keys.description: The keybinding to display: full key names, `+` joins keys into a chord (`"Mod+k"`), a space joins chords into a sequence (`"a b"`); `"Mod"` means the platform's primary modifier.

format.description: Display format: `condensed` shows compact symbols for menus and tooltips; `full` spells keys out for prose.

variant.description: Color treatment matching the surface behind the hint: `onEmphasis` for emphasis-colored backgrounds, `onPrimary` for primary buttons.

size.description: Size of the hint.

Stack.description: Arranges child components in a row or column with configurable spacing, alignment, wrapping, and padding.

children.description: The components arranged inside the stack.

direction.description: The axis children are laid out along.

align.description: Alignment of children across the layout axis.

justify.description: Distribution of children along the layout axis.

wrap.description: Whether children are forced onto one line or may wrap onto multiple lines.

padding.description: Padding inside the container on all sides.

paddingBlock.description: Vertical padding; overrides the vertical axis of `padding` when both are set.

paddingInline.description: Horizontal padding; overrides the horizontal axis of `padding` when both are set.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the container; the choices are display-equivalent flex containers that differ only in semantic/landmark identity.

StackItem.description: Wraps a single child within a stack to control whether it grows to fill or shrinks to fit the available space.

children.description: The components wrapped by this item.

grow.description: Whether the item expands to fill available space along the stack axis.

shrink.description: Whether the item shrinks to fit the available space along the stack axis.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the item wrapper; `li` is included because a stack item is the natural list element.

LabelGroup.description: Groups a set of labels or tokens together, optionally truncating the group to a maximum number of visible items with the remainder revealed on demand.

children.description: The labels or tokens grouped together.

overflowStyle.description: How items hidden by truncation are revealed — inline after the visible items, or in an overlay.

visibleChildCount.description: How many items to show before truncating; `'auto'` fits to the container width; omit to never truncate.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the group container.

AvatarStack.description: A set of overlapping avatars displayed inline, used to show a group of users or entities compactly when space is limited.

children.description: The avatars to display in the stack.

alignRight.description: Aligns the stacked avatars toward the right edge instead of the left.

disableExpand.description: Prevents the avatars from spreading apart to reveal themselves when the stack is hovered or focused.

variant.description: The overlap style; `cascade` increases the overlap for the third and later avatars, while `stack` keeps a uniform overlap.

shape.description: The shape applied to all avatars in the stack; `square` marks non-human entities such as organizations, teams, or bots.

size.description: The width and height of each avatar in the stack, in pixels; when unset, follows the individual avatars' own sizes.

ButtonGroup.description: Groups a set of related buttons into a single visually-joined row, optionally presented as a keyboard-navigable toolbar.

children.description: The buttons grouped together.

role.description: The role of the group. `toolbar` presents the buttons as a toolbar with arrow-key navigation between them; `group` marks them as a set of related controls.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the group container.

Pagination.description: Navigates a paginated collection with numbered page links and previous/next controls.

currentPage.description: The currently-selected page (1-indexed). Two-way bound: selecting a page writes the new number back to the bound data-model path.

marginPageCount.description: How many pages are always shown at the start and end.

showPages.description: Whether numbered page buttons are shown; when off, only the previous and next controls appear.

surroundingPageCount.description: How many pages are shown on each side of the current page.

hrefBuilder.description: A URL template for each page link; the token `{page}` is replaced with the page number. Provide a real URL to make pages navigable links; the default is an on-surface anchor.

accessibility.description: Label for the pagination navigation landmark, distinguishing it when a surface has more than one paginated region.

SegmentedControl.description: A set of buttons where exactly one is selected at a time, used to switch between related views or modes in a compact space.

children.description: The segments contained in the control.

selectedIndex.description: The zero-based index of the currently-selected segment; updated as the user selects.

action.description: An optional effect performed when the selection changes — a local function or a server event.

fullWidth.description: Whether the control stretches to fill its container's width.

size.description: The size of the segments.

variant.description: How the control adapts in tight spaces, per viewport: hide the labels, or collapse the whole control into a dropdown.

accessibility.description: Accessible label for the group of segments.

SegmentedControlButton.description: A single text-labeled segment within a segmented control.

label.description: The visible text label of the segment.

leadingVisual.description: An icon displayed before the label.

count.description: An optional count displayed after the label.

disabled.description: Whether the segment is disabled and cannot be selected.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies, beyond the visible label.

SegmentedControlIconButton.description: A single icon-only segment within a segmented control.

accessibility.description: Accessible label for the icon-only segment; must include a label (it renders no visible text).

description.description: Supplementary text shown in a tooltip in place of the label.

tooltipDirection.description: The direction the tooltip opens relative to the segment.

disabled.description: Whether the segment is disabled and cannot be selected.

Details.description: A disclosure that reveals or hides a section of content behind a summary the user clicks to expand or collapse.

summary.description: The always-visible label the user clicks to toggle the disclosure.

children.description: The collapsible content revealed when the disclosure is expanded.

open.description: Whether the disclosure is expanded. Two-way bound: a summary toggle or an outside-click writes the new state back to the bound path.

closeOnOutsideClick.description: Whether the disclosure collapses when the user clicks outside it.

onClickOutside.description: The action performed when the user clicks outside the open disclosure.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for the disclosure.

Select.description: A dropdown control for choosing a single option from a predefined list.

children.description: The selectable options and option groups shown in the dropdown.

value.description: The selected option's value. Two-way bound: a user's selection writes the new value back to the bound data-model path.

placeholder.description: Text shown as a non-selectable leading option before any choice is made.

disabled.description: Whether the control is inactive and cannot be changed.

required.description: Whether a selection must be made; conveyed to assistive technologies.

validationStatus.description: Validity state conveyed to assistive technologies and shown visually (a coloured border).

block.description: Whether the control expands to fill the container's full width.

size.description: The control's size.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies — the control renders no visible label of its own.

SelectOption.description: A single selectable option within a dropdown, carrying a visible label and an underlying value.

value.description: The option's underlying value, matched against the select's selected value.

disabled.description: Whether this option cannot be selected.

SelectOptGroup.description: A labelled grouping of related options within a dropdown.

children.description: The options contained in this group.

disabled.description: Whether every option in this group is disabled.

Breadcrumbs.description: A navigation trail showing the user's location within a hierarchy, as a row of links from the root to the current page.

children.description: The crumbs shown in the trail.

overflow.description: How the trail handles crumbs that don't fit: `wrap` flows them onto extra lines; `menu` and `menu-with-root` collapse the overflow into a dropdown menu.

variant.description: The visual density; `spacious` uses increased padding and a more relaxed layout.

BreadcrumbsItem.description: A single crumb within a breadcrumb trail — a link to one level of the hierarchy, or the current page.

label.description: The visible text of the crumb.

href.description: The URL this crumb navigates to when clicked. Optional — the current-page crumb may be non-navigating.

selected.description: Whether this crumb is the current page; shown as the active crumb and conveyed to assistive technologies (`aria-current="page"`).

target.description: Whether the crumb opens in the same tab (`_self`) or a new tab (`_blank`).

accessibility.description: Accessibility label/description for assistive technologies, beyond the visible crumb text.

NavList.description: A vertical list of navigation links for the current context, indicating which page is active.

children.description: The navigation items, groups, and dividers arranged in the list.

aria-label.description: An accessible name for the navigation region.

aria-labelledby.description: The id of an element that labels the navigation region.

NavList.Item.description: A single navigation link in the list; it holds the label and any leading/trailing visual, description, sub-navigation, or trailing action.

children.description: The item's label plus any leading/trailing visual, description, sub-navigation, or trailing action.

href.description: The URL the item links to.

aria-current.description: Marks this item as the user's current page or location.

defaultOpen.description: Whether the item's sub-navigation starts expanded on first render.

inactiveText.description: Text explaining why the item is inactive; its presence disables activation and shows the explanation.

NavList.SubNav.description: The nested navigation items shown when a parent item is expanded.

children.description: The nested navigation items shown when the parent item is expanded.

NavList.LeadingVisual.description: A visual element, such as an icon, shown before the item's label.

children.description: A visual element, such as an icon, shown before the item's label.

NavList.TrailingVisual.description: A visual element, such as an icon or a count, shown after the item's label to convey auxiliary information.

children.description: A visual element, such as an icon or a count, shown after the item's label.

NavList.TrailingAction.description: An interactive icon control at the end of a navigation item, separate from the navigation link, that performs an action when activated.

icon.description: The icon displayed in the control.

accessibility.description: Label for assistive technologies — the control is icon-only, so it must be labeled. Maps to `aria-label`.

action.description: The action performed when the control is activated.

loading.description: Whether the control is in a loading state.

NavList.Group.description: A container that groups related navigation items under a heading.

children.description: The related navigation items contained in the group.

NavList.GroupHeading.description: A semantic heading for a group of navigation items.

variant.description: The heading's visual style; `filled` is superimposed on a background, `subtle` is less offset.

auxiliaryText.description: Secondary text providing additional information about the group.

visuallyHidden.description: Whether the heading is hidden visually but kept available to assistive technologies.

as.description: The heading level in the document outline.

NavList.Divider.description: A visual separator between groups of navigation items.

NavList.Description.description: Supplementary text shown for a navigation item.

text.description: The supplementary text shown for the item.

variant.description: Whether the text sits beside (`inline`) or below (`block`) the item's label.

truncate.description: Whether inline text is truncated when it overflows.

NavList.GroupExpand.description: An expandable group that reveals its navigation items progressively behind a "show more" control.

items.description: The navigation items revealed as the group expands.

href.description: The URL the item links to.

ariaCurrent.description: Marks this item as the current page or location.

defaultOpen.description: Whether the item's sub-navigation starts expanded.

inactiveText.description: Text explaining why the item is inactive.

leadingVisual.description: An icon shown before the item's label.

trailingVisual.description: An octicon name or literal text shown after the item's label.

trailingAction.description: A trailing action control on the item; `action ← onClick`.

icon.description: An icon shown in the trailing action control.

label.description: Accessible label for the trailing action control.

action.description: The action performed when the control is activated.

loading.description: Whether the control is in a loading state.

label.description: Accessible name for the expand control.

pages.description: How many pages the items are revealed across.

PageLayout.description: The top-level page structure that arranges a header, main content, a supplementary pane, a full-height sidebar, and a footer.

header.description: The banner region across the top of the page.

pane.description: A supplementary region beside the content.

sidebar.description: A full-height region alongside the header, content, and footer.

footer.description: The contentinfo region across the bottom of the page.

containerWidth.description: Maximum width of the page container.

padding.description: Spacing between the container's outer edges and the viewport.

PageLayout.Header.description: The banner region at the top of a page layout.

children.description: The content placed in the header.

divider.description: Divider shown between the header and adjacent regions; supports a per-viewport value with a filled style on narrow viewports.

hidden.description: Whether the header is hidden; supports a per-viewport value.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the banner landmark.

PageLayout.Content.description: The main content region of a page layout.

children.description: The main content.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the content region; the choices are display-equivalent and differ only in semantic/landmark identity.

width.description: Maximum width of the content region.

hidden.description: Whether the content region is hidden; supports a per-viewport value.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the main landmark.

PageLayout.Pane.description: A supplementary region displayed beside the content.

children.description: The content placed in the pane.

position.description: Which side of the content the pane appears on; supports a per-viewport value.

width.description: Pane width; a named size or a custom min/default/max object.

divider.description: Divider between the pane and content; supports a per-viewport value with a filled style on narrow viewports.

sticky.description: Whether the pane sticks to the top of the viewport while scrolling.

offsetHeader.description: Top offset for a sticky pane, to clear a sticky header (a pixel number or a CSS length).

hidden.description: Whether the pane is hidden; supports a per-viewport value.

resizable.description: Whether the user can resize the pane.

currentWidth.description: The pane's controlled width in pixels. Two-way bound: a resize drag writes the new width back to the bound path.

widthStorageKey.description: Key used to persist the pane width in the browser when the width is not bound.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the pane region.

PageLayout.Sidebar.description: A full-height region alongside the header, content, and footer.

children.description: The content placed in the sidebar.

position.description: Which side of the page the sidebar appears on.

width.description: Sidebar width; a named size or a custom min/default/max object.

minWidth.description: Minimum sidebar width when resizable, in pixels.

divider.description: Divider between the sidebar and the layout.

sticky.description: Whether the sidebar sticks to the viewport while scrolling.

responsiveVariant.description: Behavior on narrow viewports; `fullscreen` expands to cover the viewport like an overlay.

hidden.description: Whether the sidebar is hidden; supports a per-viewport value.

resizable.description: Whether the user can resize the sidebar.

currentWidth.description: The sidebar's controlled width in pixels. Two-way bound: a resize drag writes the new width back to the bound path.

widthStorageKey.description: Key used to persist the sidebar width in the browser when the width is not bound; when omitted, persistence is disabled.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the sidebar region.

PageLayout.Footer.description: The contentinfo region at the bottom of a page layout.

children.description: The content placed in the footer.

divider.description: Divider shown between the footer and adjacent regions; supports a per-viewport value with a filled style on narrow viewports.

hidden.description: Whether the footer is hidden; supports a per-viewport value.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the contentinfo landmark.

SplitPageLayout.description: A page layout with a fixed split arrangement — a full-width header and footer, a main content area, and an adjacent side panel or full-height sidebar.

header.description: The banner region across the top of the page.

pane.description: The side panel region beside the main content.

sidebar.description: The full-height side region running alongside the header, content, and footer.

footer.description: The footer region across the bottom of the page.

SplitPageLayout.Header.description: The banner region across the top of a split page layout.

children.description: The content rendered inside the header region.

padding.description: Inner padding of the header region.

divider.description: A divider drawn along the edge separating the header from the content.

hidden.description: Whether the header region is hidden.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the header landmark.

SplitPageLayout.Content.description: The main content region of a split page layout.

children.description: The main content rendered inside the content region.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the content region; the choices are display-equivalent and differ only in semantic/landmark identity.

width.description: The maximum width of the content region.

padding.description: Inner padding of the content region.

hidden.description: Whether the content region is hidden.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the main landmark.

SplitPageLayout.Pane.description: A side panel region beside the main content of a split page layout, optionally resizable by the user.

children.description: The content rendered inside the pane.

position.description: Which side of the content the pane sits on.

width.description: The pane width — a named size, or explicit min/default/max px constraints.

minWidth.description: Minimum pane width in pixels (used with named sizes).

divider.description: A divider drawn along the edge between the pane and the content.

sticky.description: Whether the pane sticks to the viewport while the content scrolls.

offsetHeader.description: Top offset for the sticky pane, to clear a fixed header.

hidden.description: Whether the pane is hidden.

resizable.description: Whether the user can drag to resize the pane.

currentWidth.description: The controlled live width of the pane in pixels. Two-way bound: a resize writes the new width back to the bound path.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the pane region.

SplitPageLayout.Sidebar.description: A full-height side region running alongside the header, content, and footer of a split page layout, optionally resizable by the user.

children.description: The content rendered inside the sidebar.

position.description: Which side of the layout the sidebar sits on.

width.description: The sidebar width — a named size, or explicit min/default/max px constraints.

minWidth.description: Minimum sidebar width in pixels when resizable.

resizable.description: Whether the user can drag to resize the sidebar.

currentWidth.description: The controlled live width of the sidebar in pixels. Two-way bound: a resize writes the new width back to the bound path.

divider.description: A divider drawn between the sidebar and the content.

sticky.description: Whether the sidebar sticks to the viewport while the content scrolls.

responsiveVariant.description: Behavior at narrow viewports — retain the inline layout, or expand to a full-screen overlay.

hidden.description: Whether the sidebar is hidden.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the sidebar region.

SplitPageLayout.Footer.description: The footer region across the bottom of a split page layout.

children.description: The content rendered inside the footer region.

padding.description: Inner padding of the footer region.

divider.description: A divider drawn along the edge separating the footer from the content.

hidden.description: Whether the footer region is hidden.

accessibility.description: Accessibility label for the footer landmark.

PageHeader.description: The heading region at the top of a page — carries the page title and the contextual navigation, actions, and supporting metadata around it.

children.description: The regions and content arranged inside the page header.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the header; selects its landmark identity.

role.description: An ARIA role overriding the header's default landmark role.

hasBorder.description: Whether a divider border is shown below the header.

aria-label.description: An accessible label for the header region, for assistive technologies.

PageHeader.ContextArea.description: A container for contextual navigation and actions shown above the title, visible on narrow viewports by default.

children.description: The contextual navigation and actions shown above the title.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: hidden on regular and wide, visible on narrow only.

PageHeader.ParentLink.description: A link that navigates up to the parent page, shown above the title on narrow viewports.

href.description: The URL of the parent page the link navigates to when clicked.

aria-label.description: An accessible label for the link, for assistive technologies.

hidden.description: Whether the link is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: hidden on regular and wide, visible on narrow only.

PageHeader.ContextBar.description: A container for additional contextual content shown in the context area, visible on narrow viewports by default.

children.description: Additional contextual content shown in the context area.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: hidden on regular and wide, visible on narrow only.

PageHeader.ContextAreaActions.description: A container for action controls shown in the context area, visible on narrow viewports by default.

children.description: Action controls shown in the context area.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: hidden on regular and wide, visible on narrow only.

PageHeader.TitleArea.description: The container for the page title and the visuals and actions flanking it, with a selectable title size.

children.description: The title and the visuals and actions flanking it.

variant.description: The title's size — `subtitle`, `medium`, or `large` — optionally per viewport width.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.LeadingAction.description: A slot for an action control placed before the title, visible on regular and wide viewports by default.

children.description: An action control placed before the title.

hidden.description: Whether the slot is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: hidden on narrow, visible on regular and wide.

PageHeader.LeadingVisual.description: A slot for a visual element, such as an icon, placed before the title.

children.description: A visual element, such as an icon, placed before the title.

hidden.description: Whether the slot is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.Title.description: The page's main heading text, with a selectable semantic level.

as.description: The heading element to render; selects the semantic level in the document outline.

hidden.description: Whether the title is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.TrailingVisual.description: A slot for a visual element, such as a label or badge, placed after the title.

children.description: A visual element, such as a label or badge, placed after the title.

hidden.description: Whether the slot is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.TrailingAction.description: A slot for an action control placed after the title, visible on regular and wide viewports by default.

children.description: An action control placed after the title.

hidden.description: Whether the slot is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: hidden on narrow, visible on regular and wide.

PageHeader.Actions.description: A container for the primary action controls aligned to the end of the title area.

children.description: The primary action controls aligned to the end of the title area.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.Breadcrumbs.description: A slot for a breadcrumb trail showing the page's position in the navigation hierarchy.

children.description: The breadcrumb trail showing the page's position in the navigation hierarchy.

hidden.description: Whether the slot is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.Description.description: A container for supporting text or metadata shown below the title.

children.description: Supporting text or metadata shown below the title.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

PageHeader.Navigation.description: A container for tab-like navigation shown below the title.

children.description: The navigation items shown below the title.

as.description: The HTML element used to render the region; `nav` marks it as a navigation landmark.

aria-label.description: An accessible label for the navigation landmark, for assistive technologies.

aria-labelledby.description: The id of an element that labels the navigation landmark for assistive technologies.

hidden.description: Whether the region is hidden, optionally per viewport width (`narrow`/`regular`/`wide`). Unset applies the library default: visible on all viewports.

TreeView.description: A hierarchical list of items that expand and collapse to reveal nested items, for navigating tree-structured content such as a file system.

children.description: The tree items (rows) contained in the tree.

flat.description: Whether items render without nested indentation guidelines.

truncate.description: Whether long item labels are truncated to a single line instead of wrapping.

accessibility.description: Accessible label for the tree, for assistive technologies.

TreeViewItem.description: A single row in a tree — a label with optional leading/trailing visuals, an expandable nested subtree, and a row of secondary actions.

children.description: The item's label plus its leading/trailing visuals and any nested subtree.

expanded.description: Whether the item's subtree is open; two-way bound — toggling the item writes the new state back.

current.description: Whether this is the current item (e.g. the selected file); at most one item is current.

action.description: An effect performed when the item is activated — a local function or a server event.

secondaryActions.description: Inline trailing actions for the item, each an icon-button with a label, an optional count, and an action.

icon.description: The ID of an Icon component rendered as the action's button. Reference the component; do not define it inline.

count.description: An optional count displayed alongside the action.

action.description: The effect performed when the action is activated.

containIntrinsicSize.description: A layout-size hint used to optimize rendering of very large trees.

TreeViewSubTree.description: The nested group of items revealed when a tree item is expanded, with loading and error states for asynchronously-loaded contents.

children.description: The nested tree items revealed when the parent item is expanded.

state.description: The load state of the subtree's contents; drives the loading-skeleton and error affordances.

count.description: The number of skeleton placeholder rows shown while loading.

accessibility.description: Accessible label for the nested group, for assistive technologies.

TreeViewLeadingVisual.description: An icon shown before a tree item's label.

child.description: The icon shown before the item's label.

label.description: Accessible label describing the visual, for assistive technologies.

TreeViewTrailingVisual.description: An icon shown after a tree item's label.

child.description: The icon shown after the item's label.

label.description: Accessible label describing the visual, for assistive technologies.

TreeViewDirectoryIcon.description: A preset folder icon for a tree item that reflects the item's expanded state (open when expanded, closed when collapsed).

TreeViewErrorDialog.description: A dialog shown when a tree item's contents fail to load, offering the option to retry or dismiss.

children.description: The error message content shown in the dialog.

retryAction.description: An effect performed when the user chooses to retry — a local function or a server event.

dismissAction.description: An effect performed when the user dismisses the dialog.

UnderlineNav.description: A horizontal row of navigation tabs indicating the current page with an underline.

children.description: The navigation items arranged in the tab row.

aria-label.description: An accessible name for the navigation region.

loadingCounters.description: Whether the items' counters are loading; all counters show a loading state until every count resolves.

variant.description: Whether items are offset from the container edges (`inset`) or flush with them (`flush`).

UnderlineNav.Item.description: A single navigation tab; navigates via its link or performs an action when selected, and can mark itself the current page.

action.description: The action performed when the tab is selected by click or keyboard.

href.description: The URL the tab navigates to.

aria-current.description: Marks this tab as the user's current page or location.

leadingVisual.description: A visual rendered before the tab's label, typically an icon.

counter.description: The count displayed in a counter label after the tab's label.

Timeline.description: A vertical list of events connected by a line, each entry marked by a badge on the line and carrying its message content.

children.description: The timeline's entries — items and breaks, in display order.

clipSidebar.description: Which ends of the connecting line are clipped — `true` or `'both'` hides the line above the first and below the last entry; `'start'` or `'end'` clips only that end.

TimelineItem.description: A single entry on the timeline — a badge on the connecting line plus its body content, with an optional avatar and action controls.

children.description: The entry's badge and body, plus an optional avatar and action controls.

condensed.description: Whether the entry renders with reduced vertical padding and no badge background.

TimelineBadge.description: The marker on the timeline's connecting line for one entry — a small circle holding an icon, colored by variant.

child.description: The icon shown inside the badge circle.

variant.description: The color variant of the badge.

TimelineBody.description: The message content of a timeline entry, shown beside its badge.

children.description: The entry's message content.

TimelineBreak.description: A decorative separator marking a break between groups of timeline entries.

Dialog.description: A modal window overlaid on the page for focused tasks such as confirming an action, asking for disambiguation, or presenting a small form; it traps focus and blocks interaction with the page beneath until closed.

title.description: The dialog's heading; also its accessible name.

subtitle.description: Secondary text rendered below the title; also the accessible description.

children.description: The dialog's body content; may include header/body/footer slot components to replace those sections.

open.description: Whether the dialog is shown. Two-way bound: a dismissal (close button, Escape, or backdrop) writes `false` back to the bound path, so the agent sees the close and can reopen by setting it `true`. Defaults to open when absent.

closeAction.description: An optional custom action performed on dismissal (the close button, the Escape key, or clicking the backdrop), fired alongside the built-in close.

footerButtons.description: The buttons rendered in the dialog's footer.

buttonType.description: The button's visual emphasis; `normal` is a legacy alias the implementation maps to `default`.

action.description: The action performed when the button is clicked.

autoFocus.description: Whether this button receives focus when the dialog opens; only the first entry marked applies.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled and cannot be clicked.

loading.description: Whether the button shows a loading state while an operation is pending.

role.description: The dialog's semantic role; `alertdialog` marks an interruption that requires a response.

width.description: The dialog's width: preset sizes (small 296px, medium 320px, large 480px, xlarge 640px) or any CSS width value.

height.description: The dialog's height: small 480px, large 640px, auto sizes to the contents.

position.description: Where the dialog appears: centered over the page or as a side sheet; narrow viewports may use a bottom sheet or fullscreen.

align.description: Vertical alignment when the dialog is centered.

DialogHeader.description: A custom header region for a dialog, replacing the default title/subtitle/close-button header.

children.description: The dialog's header content, replacing the default title/subtitle/close-button header.

TimelineActions.description: Action controls displayed with a timeline entry, such as buttons.

children.description: The action controls shown with the entry.

TimelineAvatar.description: An avatar shown beside a timeline entry's badge, identifying the actor of the event.

child.description: The avatar shown beside the entry's badge.

DialogTitle.description: The dialog's heading text, rendered inside a custom dialog header.

text.description: The dialog's heading text, shown in the header.

DialogSubtitle.description: Secondary text rendered below the dialog's title inside a custom dialog header.

text.description: Secondary text shown below the dialog's title.

DialogBody.description: A custom body region for a dialog, replacing the default scrollable body.

children.description: The dialog's scrollable body content, replacing the default body.

DialogFooter.description: A custom footer region for a dialog, replacing the default button footer.

children.description: The dialog's footer content, replacing the default button footer.

DialogButtons.description: A row of action buttons rendered inside a dialog's footer.

buttons.description: The buttons rendered as the dialog's footer actions.

buttonType.description: The button's visual emphasis; `normal` is a legacy alias the implementation maps to `default`.

action.description: The action performed when the button is clicked.

autoFocus.description: Whether this button receives focus when the dialog opens; only the first entry marked applies.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled and cannot be clicked.

loading.description: Whether the button shows a loading state while an operation is pending.

DialogCloseButton.description: An icon button that closes the dialog, rendered inside a custom dialog header.

closeAction.description: Performed when the close button is clicked.

ConfirmationDialog.description: A modal confirmation prompt with exactly two buttons — one to cancel and one to confirm an action — used to confirm a user's action before it proceeds; the confirm button can be styled to mark a destructive choice.

confirmAction.description: The action performed when the user confirms — clicks the confirm button.

cancelAction.description: The action performed when the user cancels or dismisses the dialog — the cancel button, the close button, the Escape key, or the backdrop.

title.description: The dialog's heading; usually a brief question, and also its accessible name.

cancelButtonContent.description: The label of the button that dismisses the action.

confirmButtonContent.description: The label of the button that confirms the action.

confirmButtonType.description: The confirm button's visual emphasis; `danger` marks a destructive action.

cancelButtonLoading.description: Whether the cancel button shows a loading state while an operation is pending.

confirmButtonLoading.description: Whether the confirm button shows a loading state while an operation is pending.

overrideButtonFocus.description: Which button receives focus when the dialog opens; defaults to the confirm button, or the cancel button for a destructive action.

width.description: The dialog's width: preset sizes (small 296px, medium 320px, large 480px, xlarge 640px) or any CSS width value.

height.description: The dialog's height: small 480px, large 640px, auto sizes to the contents.

children.description: The dialog's body content — the message or explanation shown above the buttons.

Popover.description: A floating content container anchored near a related element, used to bring attention to it with brief contextual content.

children.description: The popover's content — typically a single content box.

caret.description: Where the pointing arrow attaches, indicating the direction of the element the popover is anchored to.

open.description: Whether the popover is currently shown.

relative.description: Whether the popover positions in-flow relative to its parent instead of floating over the layout.

as.description: The HTML element used for the wrapper; the choices are display-equivalent and differ only in semantic/landmark identity.

PopoverContent.description: The visible content box of a popover, holding its heading, message text, and action controls.

children.description: The content shown inside the box — heading, message text, and action controls.

width.description: The width of the content box.

height.description: The height of the content box.

overflow.description: How content exceeding the box's bounds is handled.

onClickOutside.description: The action performed when a click occurs outside the box, typically to dismiss it.

accessibility.description: Accessible name, description, and role for the content region, for assistive technologies.

consoleLog.description: Logs a message to the browser console. A local client-side effect.

windowAlert.description: Displays a message in a browser alert dialog. A local client-side effect.

clearValue.description: Clears a data-model path (sets it to the empty string). A local client-side effect.
